data.title: Buy Rating Reaffirmed on Dexcom as Margin Strength and Global Patient Growth Offset U.S. CGM Concerns; Price Target Reset to $75
data.description:
Josh Jennings, an analyst from TD Cowen, maintained the Buy rating on Dexcom. The associated price target was lowered to $75.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Josh Jennings has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that signal durable growth and improving profitability at Dexcom. He notes that, despite a modest U.S. revenue shortfall that reinforced bearish worries about a slower domestic CGM market, the company delivered record worldwide new patient starts, sequential U.S. new patient growth, and solid international expansion, supporting confidence in future demand.

Jennings also highlights broad-based margin strength, with gross, operating, and EBITDA margins all topping expectations, helped by manufacturing efficiencies, logistics savings, and leverage in operating expenses. He emphasizes that management raised profitability guidance and maintains conviction that expanded CMS coverage for Type 2 patients not on insulin should reignite U.S. growth, which underpins the Buy rating even as the official price target is reset to $75.

In another report released on April 20, Goldman Sachs also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $82.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Analyst Sees Buy Opportunity in Floor & Decor After Guidance Cut, Citing Structural Advantages and Margin Upside
data.description:
William Blair analyst Phillip Blee has maintained their bullish stance on FND stock, giving a Buy rating on April 29.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Phillip Blee has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Floor & Decor’s long-term positioning despite near-term headwinds. He acknowledges that the company cut its full-year sales and earnings outlook after a soft first quarter and ongoing housing-market weakness, but views the resulting share-price pressure as an attractive entry point for investors with a longer time horizon.

He also highlights several structural positives that support future upside, including the company’s ability to take price with limited pushback, an enhanced pricing organization and tools, and competitive advantages versus pressured independents and less engaged big-box rivals. In addition, he points to rising gross-margin expectations as evidence that the business can improve profitability even in a challenging volume and macro environment, which he believes is not fully reflected in current guidance or the stock price.

In another report released on April 29, Telsey Advisory also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $80.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
